authors: Shangyuan Wu Department of Communications and New Media
raw_title: Building Legislative Legitimacy of “Fake News” Laws: A Study of How Governments May Seek to Legitimize Policy Interventions Against Fake News
title_en: Building Legislative Legitimacy of “Fake News” Laws: A Study of How Governments May Seek to Legitimize Policy Interventions Against Fake News
title_zh: 构建“假新闻”法律的立法合法性：政府如何寻求合法化打击假新闻政策干预的研究
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了政府如何通过法律手段来打击假新闻，并试图让这些法律看起来更合理和正当。它关注政府如何为自己的政策辩护，以获得公众和社会的认可。
plain_summary_en: This study explores how governments use laws to combat fake news and try to make these laws seem more reasonable and justified. It focuses on how governments justify their policies to gain public and societal acceptance.
research_direction_zh: 媒体政策与法律研究，关注政府在数字时代如何通过立法手段应对虚假信息，并构建政策合法性。
research_direction_en: Media policy and legal studies, focusing on how governments use legislative measures to address disinformation in the digital age and build policy legitimacy.
